narrative.overview: Ultron CLI is designed to evoke the feeling of a futuristic, high-performance hacking terminal. It rejects the generic grey/white plain text of traditional UNIX tools in favor of a vibrant, cyberpunk-styled dashboard that feels alive and highly engineered, while maintaining near-zero shell startup latency.
keyCharacteristics: Vibrant Cyberpunk Contrast: Neon green, cyan, and pink highlight key interface states.
Micro-Animated Transitions: Smooth, high-performance loading spinners for asynchronous symlink operations.
Strict POSIX Segregation: Pure shell commands output to stdout; all design aesthetics and statuses reside in stderr.

rules.name: The 10% Color Accent Rule
rules.body: Neon colors (Green/Cyan/Pink) must never occupy more than 10% of the terminal line space.

rules.name: The Box-drawing Border Rule
rules.body: Use single-line Unicode box-drawing characters for structural tables, and avoid mixing ASCII and Unicode borders on the same screen.

dos: Do route all styled text, spinners, emojis, and status messages to stderr (console.error).
Do route raw variables, exports, and command strings directly to stdout (console.log) for shell eval parsing.
Do strip ANSI escape codes automatically when running in non-TTY environments.
Do output clean, standard ANSI color codes that adapt naturally to light and dark terminal themes.
donts: Don't output any human-readable status, emoji, or styling to stdout.
Don't block interactive terminal loops inside processes.
Don't use slow loading scripts or dependencies that raise CLI execution time above 50ms.
Don't use hardcoded background colors that could clash with the user's terminal background theme.
